meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Iran, proxy militias threaten US universities in Lebanon as Americans urged to flee now
items.summary: Iran and its proxy militias have threatened universities in Lebanon, U.S. officials say, and the State Department warned Americans to depart immediately.

items.title: Cuba releases 2,000 prisoners amid Trump pressure, energy crisis
items.summary: Cuba's government releases over 2,000 prisoners citing good behavior and health conditions as U.S. sanctions worsen the island's economic crisis.

items.title: Iran Guards recruiting children as young as 12, putting them on front lines of war
items.summary: Human Rights Watch and Amnesty International report Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps is recruiting children as young as 12 into military-linked combat and patrol roles.

items.title: Iran's tallest bridge collapses after reported US airstrikes; Iran threatens American allies in retaliation
items.summary: Iran's biggest bridge near Tehran crashes down after reported U.S. airstrikes as Trump presses the regime to make a deal before escalation.

items.title: Jewish communities on high alert as Passover begins amid rising security threats nationwide
items.summary: Jewish communities face a heightened security threat this Passover, with NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch warning of an elevated alert as antisemitic attacks rise across the U.S.

items.title: Nepal probe exposes $20M fraudulent rescue scheme involving lacing Mt Everest hikers' food: report
items.summary: Insurance companies are being scammed out of money by fraudulent rescue schemes in the nation of Nepal, according to a report by The Kathmandu Post.

items.title: Iran regime uses war to mask 'brutal' execution surge against political opponents
items.summary: A State Department spokesperson called Iran's executions "barbaric" as the United Nations and activists group report the regime is using the death penalty to crush dissent.

items.title: Israeli paramedic delivers baby, rushes it to bomb shelter during Iran attack
items.summary: Israeli paramedic Dr. Gal Rosen delivered a baby in Tel Aviv, then raced to a bomb shelter with the newborn as Iron Dome missile sirens blared overhead.

items.title: Ukraine signals progress on US security guarantees after call with Trump envoys
items.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says talks with Trump envoys Witkoff and Kushner showed progress on Ukraine security guarantees, calling for a leaders' summit to advance peace.

items.title: Nigeria's Christians on edge for Easter after Palm Sunday massacre
items.summary: Nigeria, ranked seventh-worst in the world for Christian persecution, saw a reported 28 killed on Palm Sunday, with fears Christians could be targeted this Easter.

items.title: Iran's ceasefire push may be a 'cycle of deception,' analysts warn as shadowy figure gains power
items.summary: Iran's shadowy Ahmad Vahidi, a former Quds Force commander linked to deadly bombings abroad, is now more dominant than Iran's president or Khamenei's son, experts warn.

items.title: Turkey’s NATO role under scrutiny amid new report on Hamas, Muslim Brotherhood ties
items.summary: A new Foundation for Defense of Democracies report alleges Turkey under Erdogan has shifted toward Islamist movements, raising NATO alliance concerns ahead of a July summit in Ankara, Turkey.

items.title: Israel approves sweeping death penalty legislation targeting terrorism, EU condemns move
items.summary: Israel's Knesset voted to mandate the death penalty for terrorists convicted of deadly attacks, a shift lawmakers say was driven by the horrors of the Oct. 7 massacre.

items.title: Russian military plane crash in Crimea kills 29 people
items.summary: A Russian military transport plane crashed in annexed Crimea, killing 29 people aboard as officials pointed to a possible technical malfunction.

items.title: Exclusive: Sara Netanyahu warns of surging antisemitism and importance of Jewish-Christian alliance
items.summary: Sara Netanyahu wrapped up a four-day Washington visit at the invitation of the first lady Melania Trump focused on tech and education over the backdrop of the Iran war and rising antisemitism.

items.title: Iran's 'basement' Chinese drone networks spark fears of sleeper cell attacks on US soil
items.summary: Draganfly's Cameron Chell warns Iran could produce over 100,000 FPV drones monthly, with swarm attacks on U.S. soil possible within eight months.

items.title: Iran moderates pushing Trump deal risk being ‘eliminated’ as regime fractures deepen
items.summary: Pete Hegseth says Iran should make a deal to end the war as Secretary of State Marco Rubio cites internal fractures in the new Iranian regime.

items.title: Gunmen on bikes storm Nigeria village on Palm Sunday, killing at least 20
items.summary: Gunmen on motorcycles shot sporadically into a Nigerian community on Palm Sunday, killing at least 20, residents say, prompting a 48-hour curfew in Plateau state.

items.title: More key US allies block military flights as Iran war rift widens with Trump
items.summary: President Donald Trump blasted European allies on Truth Social, criticizing their refusal to support U.S. military operations tied to the war with Iran.

items.title: King Charles to visit US in landmark trip marking America's 250th birthday
items.summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla will visit the U.S. next month, marking his first state visit as king and the 250th anniversary of independence.

items.title: Russia allegedly sharing satellite intelligence on US bases with Iran, world leader claims
items.summary: Zelenskyy warns Russian satellites photographed U.S. military bases across the Middle East in Iran's interests, signaling possible strike preparations.

items.title: Father loses legal fight to halt euthanasia of 25-year-old daughter in Spain
items.summary: Noelia Castillo Ramos' case reached the European Court of Human Rights before 25-year-old died in Barcelona on March 26, 2025.

items.title: Iran's internet blackout hiding strike damage and suppressing dissent, Israeli officials say
items.summary: Israeli officials say Iran's internet blackout is hiding war damage, jamming Starlink, and stopping civilians from organizing amid mounting regime pressure.

items.title: Iran's war against the US and Israel is being fueled by North Korean weapons, expert warns
items.summary: Iran's ballistic missiles, including the Shahab-3 and Khorramshahr-4, were developed with North Korean help, says expert Bruce Bechtol, who urges stronger sanctions enforcement.

items.title: US allows Russian oil tanker to reach Cuba amid blockade as Trump says island ‘has to survive’
items.summary: The U.S. is reportedly allowing a Russian oil tanker to reach Cuba as President Donald Trump eases pressure on the island, saying country "needs to survive."
